_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second research pass) - see ISSUES.md ISS-9/data-requirements.md §17 before treating any value here as final
_note: Holds financial-model assumptions that are NOT equipment-specific (SPEC.md §11.1.F / §18) — same value would otherwise be repeated identically across every equipment-data/*.json file. Equipment-specific benchmarks (purchase cost, useful life, utilization, tariff, launch delay) stay in the per-equipment files, not here.

discountRate.note: Proxy from listed-Indian-hospital-chain WACC (S22: HCG 12.2%, KMC 12.1%, Apollo 12.4%, Fortis 14.1%, Narayana 12.4%, Max 13.5%) plus one analyst DCF assumption (S23: 11.1%). Not an equipment-project-specific discount rate -- a company-level proxy. See data-requirements.md §17.1. typical=12.5 is the rounded midpoint of the 12-13% cluster, not itself a quoted figure.

targetIrr.note: Confirmed unresearchable across two research passes (data-requirements.md §17.2) -- no Indian hospital/investor hurdle-rate benchmark is publicly disclosed anywhere. Suggested UI guidance (a methodology, not a benchmark): offer discount rate + 300-500bps as a starting-point heuristic the user can override, clearly labeled as a suggestion, not a researched number. **Decision (2026-07-12, UI assurance audit F1, Jay's call):** the wizard auto-fills targetIrr with discountRate + 400bps (midpoint of this range) as an editable 'Typical'-tagged value, per content/inputs-metadata.json -- this value stays 'Unavailable' here as the honest research-confidence record; the heuristic is a UI-level default, not a claimed benchmark, and is never presented as sourced.

loanInterestRate.note: S11 (Bajaj) gives up to 14% p.a.; S12 (Tata Capital) gives a broad 8-15% market range. data-requirements.md recommends this as sensitivity_range, not a clean single default — 11.5 is a derived midpoint of the cited ranges, not a quoted figure. UI must show it as an editable range with this confidence, not as a silent single default, and must prefer the user's actual lender quote per data-requirements.md §7.3.

loanTenureMonths.note: S10 (HDFC) gives 12-84 months; S11 (Bajaj) gives 3-120 months. 60 is a derived midpoint within both ranges, not a directly quoted figure.

workingDaysPerMonth.confidence: N/A - modeling convention, not a researched benchmark
workingDaysPerMonth.note: Generic calendar assumption (25 working days assumes Sunday closure), classified as a 'safe default' under data-requirements.md §7.1, not a sourced benchmark. Always editable; do not present as researched.
